page: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family. Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, this instance provides 2 vCPUs mapped from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It features no local storage, requiring external storage solutions, and is premium storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results highlight a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The D2als_v6 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for both stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core tests. However, due to its low core count, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is also constrained, with read-only bandwidth and PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 25%, while database operations on PassMark register in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average tier performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D2als_v6 offers a low-memory, entry-level compute profile. It is designed for workloads that require dedicated CPU cycles with high single-core clock speeds but do not demand large memory footprints or local storage. This makes the instance suitable for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and simple text processing tasks. It is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases, parallelized compilation, or complex multi-threaded operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. Configured with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, this instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core execution, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. Conversely, its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reside in the bottom 25%, with database operations scoring in the bottom 10%. Qualitatively, the D2als_v6 represents a cost-efficient option for entry-level compute tasks that prioritize single-threaded performance over memory capacity. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and simple text processing, while being poorly matched for memory-heavy databases or parallelized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a Dalv6-family virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance but has limited multi-core and memory capacity.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a Dalv6 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A low-memory, entry-level compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC single-core performance for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
